description: Defines how raw data should be read into the structured form.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

separator.description: Sets a single character as a separator for each field and value.

encoding.description: Decodes the CSV files by the given encoding type.

quote.description: Sets a single character used for escaping quoted values where the separator can be part of the value. Set an empty string to turn off quotations.

escape.description: Sets a single character used for escaping quotes inside an already quoted value.

header.description: Use the first line as names of columns.

inferSchema.description: Infers the input schema automatically from data. It requires one extra pass over the data.

nullValue.description: Sets the string representation of a null value.

dateFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a date format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

timestampFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a timestamp format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

Json.description: Reader for JSON files that contain an array of objects within them.

subPath.description: Path in the form of `a.b.c` to a sub-element of the root JSON object that is an array or objects. If not specified it is assumed that the root element is an array.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

dateFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a date format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

encoding.description: Allows to forcibly set one of standard basic or extended encodings.

timestampFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a timestamp format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

NdJson.description: Reader for files containing multiple newline-delimited JSON objects with the same schema.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

dateFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a date format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

encoding.description: Allows to forcibly set one of standard basic or extended encodings.

timestampFormat.description: Sets the string that indicates a timestamp format. The `rfc3339` is the only required format, the other format strings are implementation-specific.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

GeoJson.description: Reader for GeoJSON files. It expects one `FeatureCollection` object in the root and will create a record per each `Feature` inside it extracting the properties into individual columns and leaving the feature geometry in its own column.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

NdGeoJson.description: Reader for Newline-delimited GeoJSON files. It is similar to `GeoJson` format but instead of `FeatureCollection` object in the root it expects every individual feature object to appear on its own line.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

ddlSchema.description: DEPRECATED: A DDL-formatted schema. Schema can be used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.

subPath.description: If the ZIP archive contains multiple shapefiles use this field to specify a sub-path to the desired `.shp` file. Can contain glob patterns to act as a filter.

schema.description: Schema used to coerce values into more appropriate data types.
